text.title: Rust Project Structure (Axum/Actix) Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules for rust-project-structure. Each rule pairs binary check + sourced rationale. Last rule is the terminal skip-this-methodology guard.

rule.statement: Organize Rust HTTP services under src/ with: config.rs, error.rs, and domain-split directories: routes/, handlers/, services/, models/, db/, middleware/. Keep main.rs under ~80 lines (config load, state build, router compose, serve).
rule.rationale: A flat src/ with all code in main.rs hits the 500-line wall quickly and causes circular mod.rs imports. The split enforces a one-way dependency: routes use handlers; handlers use services; services use db; models are shared.

rule.statement: AppState must derive Clone. Wrap all non-trivially-Clone resources (DB pool, HTTP client, Config) in Arc. Never put &amp;amp;amp;amp;str, non-Send types, or borrowed data in AppState.
rule.rationale: Axum clones AppState per request. Arc wrapping keeps the clone O(1) (atomic increment). Non-Clone or non-Send types cause compile errors that agents diagnose poorly.

rule.statement: Define per-domain route functions (e.g. users::routes()) that return Router&amp;amp;amp;lt;AppState&amp;amp;amp;gt; without state. Compose them in a top-level api_routes() function. Attach state and layers only once at the top level in main.rs.
rule.rationale: Attaching state inside domain route functions makes them order-sensitive and hard to test independently. The top-level composition point is the single place where AppState is bound.

rule.statement: Promote to a Cargo workspace when: (a) cargo check takes &amp;amp;amp;gt;30s, or (b) a second binary (worker, admin CLI) is needed, or (c) a shared-types crate is required. Single-crate is correct until then.
rule.rationale: Premature workspace adds build complexity. The concrete signals above are measurable; "might need it" is not.

rule.statement: Pin all dependencies to exact minor versions in Cargo.toml. Commit Cargo.lock for binary crates (not for libraries). Agents must read the existing Cargo.toml for versions before adding new deps.
rule.rationale: Axum 0.6 and 0.7 have breaking router-state changes. An agent picking the "latest" version may generate code incompatible with the rest of the codebase.

rule.statement: After creating a new .rs file in src/dir/name.rs, always add pub mod name; to src/dir/mod.rs. Verify by grepping mod.rs before reporting the task complete.
rule.rationale: A missing pub mod line produces "unresolved module" errors that are non-obvious and confuse agents into adding duplicate files.

rule.statement: Use SQLX_OFFLINE=true and commit .sqlx/ query cache files when sqlx::query! macros are used. CI must not require a running database to compile.
rule.rationale: sqlx::query! macros connect to DATABASE_URL at compile time. Without offline mode, CI fails on every PR that lacks database connectivity.

rule.statement: If the preconditions in `Applies If` fail or any of the `Skip If` conditions is true, return `skipped` and apply no further rules.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are not universal; running them outside their fit-window wastes effort and produces wrong outputs.
